title: Evidence-Aware Ideation for Open Research
abstract: Grounding ideas with retrieved evidence reduces unsupported claims in science ideation systems.
claims: Retrieved evidence improves grounding and feasibility when the corpus captures assumptions and risks.
assumptions: A frozen literature snapshot is available.
risks: Retrieval can bias the model toward conservative ideas.

title: Language Feedback for Preference Search
abstract: Natural-language critique helps search systems repair flawed proposals instead of only ranking them.
claims: Pairwise preference plus critique yields more actionable updates than win-rate alone.
assumptions: Judges can describe the main flaw clearly.
risks: Weak judges may hallucinate improvement directions.

title: Blind Pairwise Evaluation for Research Ideas
abstract: Blind pairwise judging with factor-isolated prompts improves ranking stability over pointwise scalar scoring.
claims: Blind pairwise evaluation improves agreement with human reviewers.
risks: Length bias still leaks into weak judge prompts.
